pattern: The k interleaved subsequences (indices 0,k,2k,...; 1,k+1,2k+1,...; etc.) must each become non-decreasing independently, so the minimum number of changes is each subsequence's length minus its longest-non-decreasing-subsequence, summed across all k subsequences.
hints: arr is k-increasing exactly when every one of the k interleaved subsequences (starting at offsets 0..k-1, stepping by k) is non-decreasing on its own.
For one subsequence, the fewest elements you must change equals its length minus the length of its longest non-decreasing subsequence -- everything in that longest subsequence can stay untouched.

Find the longest non-decreasing subsequence with the patience-sorting trick (binary search for the first tail strictly greater than the current value), then sum length-minus-that over all k subsequences.

edge_inputs.description: single element array, always already k-increasing
k=1, whole array must become non-decreasing
all equal values are already non-decreasing, no changes needed
k equal to the array length, every subsequence has length one
one subsequence already sorted, the other needs several changes

stress.description: random values with random k, mixing runs and reversals across residue classes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    arr = [rng.randint(1, n) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [arr, k]
